distinct-values-for-report-categories.label: Distinct Values for Report Categories
distinct-values-for-report-categories.summary: Inspect the unique values and combinations available before defining a report filter or grouping rule.

single-choice-1.title: Effect of DISTINCT
single-choice-1.hint: Think about duplicates in the selected output.

help.concept: DISTINCT keeps one copy of each selected value or row.
help.hint_1: It does not aggregate into a numeric count.
help.hint_2: The selected column determines what uniqueness means.

options.b: One row for each unique category

single-choice-2.title: Two-Column Distinct Grain
single-choice-2.prompt: What does one row represent in `SELECT DISTINCT region, category`?
single-choice-2.hint: Uniqueness applies to the complete selected row.

help.concept: With two columns, DISTINCT evaluates each pair.
help.hint_1: Either individual value may repeat in another pair.
help.hint_2: Describe the row using both selected dimensions.

options.b: One category for the entire company

multi-choice-1.title: Good Uses for DISTINCT
multi-choice-1.prompt: Which tasks are good uses of DISTINCT? Select all that apply.
multi-choice-1.hint: Choose discovery tasks rather than arithmetic summaries.

help.concept: DISTINCT is useful for exploring categories and combinations.
help.hint_1: Totals require aggregate functions rather than duplicate removal.
help.hint_2: Look for tasks asking which values exist.

options.d: Check which representatives appear in the table

multi-choice-2.title: Distinct Output Facts
multi-choice-2.prompt: Which statements are true for `SELECT DISTINCT region, sales_rep`? Select all that apply.
multi-choice-2.hint: Evaluate uniqueness at the pair level.

help.concept: DISTINCT changes the query result, not stored data.
help.hint_1: A region can pair with multiple representatives.
help.hint_2: Sorting is separate from duplicate removal.

options.a: A region may appear in several rows.
options.d: Adding ORDER BY can make the list easier to review.

drag-reorder-1.title: Write a Distinct Audit
drag-reorder-1.prompt: Arrange the clauses for a DISTINCT category audit in written SQL order.
drag-reorder-1.hint: The selected columns come before the source and sorting clauses.

help.concept: The standard written order is SELECT, FROM, then ORDER BY.

fill-blank-choice-1.title: Complete the Unique-Status Query
fill-blank-choice-1.prompt: Fill the keyword that removes repeated statuses from the result.
fill-blank-choice-1.hint: Choose the keyword that keeps one copy of each selected value.

help.concept: DISTINCT belongs between SELECT and the selected column.
help.hint_1: No aggregation is required for this audit.
help.hint_2: The query asks for unique values, not grouped totals.

try_distinct_values_for_report_categorie.title: List the Available Sales Regions
try_distinct_values_for_report_categorie.prompt: Return each `region` once and sort the regions alphabetically.
try_distinct_values_for_report_categorie.hint: Select only the column whose unique values you need.

help.concept: DISTINCT applies to the selected result rows, while ORDER BY makes the audit readable.
help.hint_2: Add alphabetical ordering after the table name.

try_distinct_values_for_report_categorie.title: Map Representatives to Regions
try_distinct_values_for_report_categorie.prompt: Return every unique `region` and `sales_rep` combination. Sort by region, then representative.
try_distinct_values_for_report_categorie.hint: DISTINCT evaluates the pair of selected columns together.

help.concept: A unique combination report can contain repeated values in either individual column.
help.hint_2: Use the same left-to-right order in ORDER BY for predictable scanning.

sketch0.title: Inspect One Business Dimension
sketch0.bodyMarkdown:
`DISTINCT` removes duplicate rows from the selected output. It is useful when you need to learn which values are available before writing a filter.

```sql
SELECT DISTINCT order_status
FROM sales_reporting
ORDER BY order_status;
```

This returns each status once. It does not delete duplicates from the table.

sketch1.title: Inspect Unique Combinations
sketch1.bodyMarkdown:
With more than one selected column, `DISTINCT` keeps one copy of each unique combination.

```sql
SELECT DISTINCT region, category
FROM sales_reporting
ORDER BY region, category;
```

A region can still appear several times because it is paired with different categories. One output row now represents one region-category combination.
